_meta.notes: Bell-keeper at Furrowmouth. Knows which day is which grain by ear before they read the chalkboard.

tagline: small, tidy, faintly always smells of grain dust; rings the bell as if it owes her something.

firstMeet.subject: rye day, and you knew it
firstMeet.body:
It was rye day when you came past. I had not yet rung the bell, and you knew. Most don't. I marked you down for an apprentice's sense, even if the apprenticeship was somewhere else, and somewhen else. 

The elven wholesalers come on the second-week morning. If you'd like to learn the calls before then, the bell-loft is open after the noon ring.

— Lis

metadata.voice: warm, direct, fond of the elven wholesalers in a way that confuses them
